name: pde_layer0_never_page
code:
proof fn phi_1_pde_layer0_never_page(entry: usize)
    requires
        entry & 1 == 1,
    ensures
        !((PDE { entry, layer: Ghost(0) })@ is Page),
{
}
reason: Layer 0 (PML4) entries should never be Page — they are always Directory or Invalid; if a Page entry appears at layer 0, the walk semantics would be wrong

name: pde_layer3_never_directory
code:
proof fn phi_2_pde_layer3_never_directory(entry: usize)
    requires
        entry & 1 == 1,
        (PDE { entry, layer: Ghost(3) }).all_mb0_bits_are_zero(),
    ensures
        !((PDE { entry, layer: Ghost(3) })@ is Directory),
{
}
reason: Layer 3 (PT) entries should never be Directory — they are always Page or Invalid; if a Directory entry appears at layer 3, the walk would recurse beyond 4 levels

name: walk_result_vbase_leq_vaddr
code:
proof fn phi_3_walk_result_vbase_leq_vaddr(w: Walk)
    requires
        w.path.last().1 is Page,
        w.path.len() >= 2,
        w.path.len() <= 4,
    ensures
        w.result() is Valid,
        w.result()->vbase <= w.vaddr,
{
}
reason: `vbase = align_to_usize(vaddr, entry_size)` rounds down, so `vbase <= vaddr` — if this fails, the alignment function is incorrectly rounding up

name: pde_layer_geq4_always_invalid
reason: Layers ≥ 4 don't exist in x86-64 — any PDE at layer 4 should be Invalid; if it's classified as Directory or Page, the walk could exceed the 4-level hierarchy
